Details:
Homogeneous oxidation (no catalyst) of RDX occurred at temperatures as low as 85° C and complete oxidation occurred at temperatures above 110° C in less than 30 minutes at a constant pressure of 500 psig [1714]. The addition of the catalyst, Pt/TiO2 had little effect at 85° C, however the catalyst nearly doubled the reaction rate at 95° C resulting in total oxidation in 30 minutes. The results show that the oxidation of RDX can occur very rapidly and is strongly temperature dependent. The impact of catalyst addition is also dependent upon temperature, with increased reaction rates due to catalyst addition occurring at higher temperatures.
These conditions are extreme compared to typical drinking water treatment and are only suggestive of catalytic TiO2 treatment at typical temperatures and pressures.
